name: Thought and Language Index
analyst_summary: TLI rates 8 specific forms of disordered thought/language on a 0.25-1.0 severity scale, organized into 3 subscales: Impoverished (Poverty of Speech, Weakening of Goal), Disorganised (Looseness, Peculiar Word Use, Peculiar Sentence Construction, Peculiar Logic), and Non-Specific Dysregulation (Perseveration, Distractibility). Each instance is counted with its severity; subscale totals capture independent dimensions per factor analysis. Healthy speech sits almost entirely at 0.25 with isolated 0.50s. Particularly useful for CoT-trace analysis where Looseness, Weakening of Goal, Peculiar Logic, and Perseveration reveal whether reasoning chains stay coherent vs. drifting or collapsing.
source: Liddle, P.F. et al. (2002). Thought and Language Index: an instrument for assessing thought and language in schizophrenia. British Journal of Psychiatry, 181(4), 326-330.
elicitation: Eight TAT pictures presented sequentially, 1 minute of speech per picture, yielding 8 speech samples

items.name: Poverty of Speech
items.definition: Decrease in the amount of speech. Responses to the picture and replies to questions are brief and lack elaboration.

severity_examples.0.25: Slightly less speech than expected; somewhat terse responses
severity_examples.0.50: Noticeably brief responses; requires prompting to continue
severity_examples.0.75: Very brief, minimal responses despite encouragement
severity_examples.1.0: Near-absence of speech; single words or silence

items.name: Weakening of Goal
items.definition: Lack of drive in thinking manifest in lack of normal elaboration of ideas, use of uninformative generalisations and empty speech with little substantive information.

severity_examples.0.50: Frequent use of empty generalisations; low information density
severity_examples.0.75: Speech largely devoid of substantive content despite adequate volume
severity_examples.1.0: Completely empty speech; no meaningful information conveyed

items.name: Looseness
items.definition: Connection between ideas is tenuous or absent, or extraneous ideas intrude into the train of thought. Parallels derailment/tangentiality in other systems.

severity_examples.0.25: Slight tangential drift that can be followed
severity_examples.0.50: Clear non-sequitur or intrusion of an extraneous idea, but speaker returns to topic
severity_examples.0.75: Multiple loose connections; difficult to follow the thread
severity_examples.1.0: Complete loss of thread; ideas appear random or disconnected

items.name: Peculiar Word Use
items.definition: Unusual or invented words are used. At mild levels, this means using a rare word when a common word would convey the meaning more clearly.

severity_examples.0.25: Using a rare word when a common word would be clearer
severity_examples.0.50: Clear unusual word choice that causes brief confusion
severity_examples.0.75: Word usage that substantially impedes comprehension
severity_examples.1.0: Neologisms or word use that prevents comprehension

items.name: Peculiar Sentence Construction
items.definition: Form of the sentences is unusual. Greater weight is given to peculiarity of sentence construction which makes it difficult to discern the meaning.

severity_examples.0.25: Mildly unusual syntax that can be parsed
severity_examples.0.50: Sentence structure that requires re-reading to understand
severity_examples.0.75: Sentence construction that substantially obscures meaning

items.name: Peculiar Logic
items.definition: Conclusions are reached based on inadequate evidence or faulty logic.

severity_examples.0.50: Clear logical gap or non-sequitur reasoning

items.name: Perseveration
items.definition: Unwarranted repetition of ideas or themes.

severity_examples.0.25: Mild repetition of a theme within expected limits
severity_examples.0.50: Clear unnecessary return to an already-covered idea
severity_examples.0.75: Persistent repetition across multiple speech samples

items.name: Distractibility
items.definition: Intrusion of extraneous ideas arising from an external stimulus.

severity_examples.0.25: Brief mention of an external stimulus before returning to task
severity_examples.0.50: Clear disruption of speech by external stimulus
severity_examples.1.0: Unable to maintain focus due to constant external intrusions

scoring_notes.healthy_controls: Scores accumulated almost entirely from items scored at 0.25, with only isolated instances of 0.50 in one or two individuals
scoring_notes.subscale_independence: The three subscale scores are approximately orthogonal based on factor analysis, capturing independent dimensions of thought disorder
scoring_notes.total_score: Sum of all individual instance scores across all items and speech samples
scoring_notes.cot_applicability: For LLM CoT traces, Looseness, Weakening of Goal, Peculiar Logic, and Perseveration are the most directly applicable categories since they do not require auditory/vocal assessment
